Import TensorFlow, Keras, KerasCV, tqdm, and xml.etree.ElementTree modules
Set hyperparameters: BATCH_SIZE=4, SPLIT_RATIO=0.2, LEARNING_RATE=0.001, EPOCH=3, GLOBAL_CLIPNORM=10.0
Define class IDs and create class_mapping dictionary with 5 classes: ['car', 'pedestrian', 'trafficLight', 'biker', 'truck']
Prepare dataset paths for images and annotations (XML files) and list all XML and JPG files sorted
Implement parse_annotation function to parse XML files extracting image paths, bounding boxes in [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] format, and class IDs mapped from class names
Iterate over all XML files to create lists: image_paths, bbox (list of boxes per image), and classes (list of class IDs per image)
Convert bbox and classes lists into ragged tensors using tf.ragged.constant; convert image_paths to ragged tensor as well
Create tf.data.Dataset from_tensor_slices with (image_paths, classes, bbox)
Split the dataset into validation and training datasets using data.take(num_val) and data.skip(num_val) with num_val = int(len(xml_files) * SPLIT_RATIO)
Define load_image function to read and decode JPEG images from image paths
Define load_dataset function to load images and package bounding boxes and classes into dictionary {'classes': casted float32 classes ragged tensor, 'boxes': bbox ragged tensor}

Create data augmentation keras.Sequential pipeline with RandomFlip (mode='horizontal'), RandomShear (x_factor=0.2, y_factor=0.2), and JitteredResize (target_size=(640, 640), scale_factor=(0.75, 1.3)) all with bounding_box_format='xyxy'
Build training dataset pipeline: map load_dataset, shuffle with buffer size BATCH_SIZE * 4, ragged_batch with size BATCH_SIZE and drop_remainder=True, then map augmenter with parallel calls

Build validation dataset pipeline: map load_dataset, shuffle with buffer size BATCH_SIZE * 4, ragged_batch with size BATCH_SIZE and drop_remainder=True, then map resizing JitteredResize layer with same config as augmenter but without flip and shear
Define dict_to_tuple function to convert dataset elements from {'images': images, 'bounding_boxes': bounding_boxes} to tuple (images, bounding_boxes)
Map dict_to_tuple to both train_ds and val_ds, followed by prefetch with tf.data.AUTOTUNE
Initialize YOLOV8 backbone using keras_cv.models.YOLOV8Backbone.from_preset with preset 'yolo_v8_s_backbone_coco'
Create YOLOV8Detector model with num_classes=len(class_mapping), bounding_box_format='xyxy', backbone as above, and fpn_depth=1
Compile the model with Adam optimizer (learning_rate=LEARNING_RATE, global_clipnorm=GLOBAL_CLIPNORM), classification_loss='binary_crossentropy', and box_loss='ciou'

Define EvaluateCOCOMetricsCallback subclass of keras.callbacks.Callback with constructor accepting validation dataset and save_path; initialize keras_cv.metrics.BoxCOCOMetrics with bounding_box_format='xyxy' and evaluate_freq=1e9; implement on_epoch_end method that resets metric state, iterates over validation batches obtaining y_true and y_pred (via model.predict), updates metric state, obtains metrics.result(force=True), updates logs, and saves model if mAP improves

Start model training by calling yolo.fit with train_ds, validation_data=val_ds, epochs=3, and callbacks list containing an instance of EvaluateCOCOMetricsCallback with val_ds and 'model.h5' as arguments

Observe and assert that after first epoch, during callback on_epoch_end, an UnknownError occurs with InvalidArgumentError in tf.concat operation, caused by mismatched dimensions along axis 1 with shapes like [4,2,4] vs [4,1,4]
Verify that the error traceback points to keras_cv.metrics.object_detection.box_coco_metrics.py in _box_concat function where tf.concat is called on bounding boxes from ground truths
Confirm that the error reproduces consistently in Colab Pro environment with the described dataset and pipeline setup
